---
created: "2026-03-22T00:00:00Z"
id: task-sv0doc-milestone-0
key: sv0doc-milestone-0
state: done
tags:
    - sv0
    - sv0doc
    - milestone-0
title: "milestone 0: extract formal language specification"
type: task
---

# milestone 0: extract formal language specification

## purpose

extract the formal sv0 language specification from the design document into
sv0doc/ as a structured, implementable reference. this is the prerequisite
for all compiler and VM work -- sv0c and sv0vm implement against the
specifications produced here.

milestone 0 is complete when:
- the grammar is formalized in EBNF
- the type system rules are documented
- the contract semantics are specified
- the memory model (ownership, borrowing, borrows()) is defined
- the keyword and operator reference is extracted
- bytecode documentation has anchor files under **sv0doc/bytecode/** (**format** + **instructions**) so **`sv0 doc`**, sv0c VM backend, and sv0vm share a normative location; **`scripts/verify_sv0doc_baseline.py`** (run from **`./scripts/sv0 test`**) asserts those paths plus the other required **sv0doc** files exist and are non-empty

## dependencies

/require: bash

## configuration

/env: SV0DOC_ROOT=sv0doc
/env: DESIGN_DOC_URL=http://development.sasankvishnubhatla.net/tcowmbh/task/sv0-compiler-vision-and-design.html

## specification extraction

the design document contains all language features as prose with code examples.
each sub-agent extracts one section into a formal, implementable format.

### step 1: formal grammar (EBNF)

/ai: extract the complete EBNF grammar for sv0 from the design document.
     cover all syntactic constructs: bindings, control flow, pattern matching,
     functions with contracts, types (struct, enum, trait, impl, newtype, type),
     modules and imports, closures, generics, and attributes.
     write the grammar to sv0doc/grammar/sv0.ebnf.

/include: task/sv0doc-extract-grammar.Rmd

### step 2: type system rules

/ai: extract the type system rules from the design document. cover:
     - primitive types and their relationships
     - type inference rules for local bindings
     - generic type constraints and trait bounds
     - trait resolution and impl validation
     - function overloading resolution
     - Copy/Clone auto-derivation rules
     - Send/Sync inference rules
     write the rules to sv0doc/type-system/rules.md.

/include: task/sv0doc-extract-type-rules.Rmd

### step 3: contract semantics

/ai: extract the contract system semantics from the design document. cover:
     - requires/ensures/loop_invariant syntax and semantics
     - the result binding in ensures clauses
     - old() for pre-state snapshots
     - forall/exists quantifiers
     - no_alias() for aliasing contracts
     - borrows() for borrow-source annotations
     - the three verification phases (runtime, local static, modular)
     - contract-mode settings (runtime, verified, disabled)
     write the semantics to sv0doc/contracts/semantics.md.

/include: task/sv0doc-extract-contracts.Rmd

### step 4: memory model

/ai: extract the memory model specification from the design document. cover:
     - ownership and move semantics
     - the exclusivity rule (&T XOR &mut T)
     - automatic local borrow tracking (tier 1)
     - elision rules at function boundaries (tier 2)
     - borrows() contracts for complex cases (tier 3)
     - structs holding references
     - unsafe blocks and raw pointers
     - Copy/Clone trait semantics
     write the specification to sv0doc/memory-model/ownership.md.

/include: task/sv0doc-extract-memory-model.Rmd

### step 5: keyword and operator reference

/ai: extract the complete keyword and operator reference tables from the
     design document. include the trait mapping for each operator.
     write the reference to sv0doc/keywords/reference.md.

/include: task/sv0doc-extract-keywords.Rmd

## validation

/assert: test -f $SV0DOC_ROOT/grammar/sv0.ebnf
/assert: test -f $SV0DOC_ROOT/type-system/rules.md
/assert: test -f $SV0DOC_ROOT/contracts/semantics.md
/assert: test -f $SV0DOC_ROOT/memory-model/ownership.md
/assert: test -f $SV0DOC_ROOT/keywords/reference.md
/assert: test -f $SV0DOC_ROOT/bytecode/format.md
/assert: test -f $SV0DOC_ROOT/bytecode/instructions.md

/ai: review the extracted specifications for completeness and consistency.
     identify any gaps or ambiguities that need resolution before sv0c
     implementation begins. list them as open questions.
